diffReviewed: HEAD (working tree, 5 files staged for commit: workflows/module-management/src/index.ts, workflows/module-management/src/design/index.ts, workflows/module-management/src/design/chat.ts, apps/cli/src/index.ts, apps/cli/src/commands/module-design/consistency.ts)

findings.description: Top-level barrel's explicit value re-export list omits `applySlashCommand`. The original barrel used `export * from "./design"`, which re-exported `applySlashCommand` (a function exported from `design/index.ts`'s `./state.js` block). The hand-written replacement list drops this name, so any consumer that does `import { applySlashCommand } from "@repo/module-management-workflow"` now gets `undefined`. This is a silent backward-compatibility regression.
findings.consequence: Any external consumer (or future in-repo caller) that imports `applySlashCommand` from the workflow package will get `undefined` and crash at call time. Today no in-repo consumer imports it through the barrel (state.test.ts and slash.test.ts import directly from `./state` and `./slash`), so the regression is latent but real.
findings.writerResolution: Fixed: added `applySlashCommand` to the value re-export block in workflows/module-management/src/index.ts. Battle-test now passes (barrel-completeness.test.ts: 2/2). Verified via independent tsx probe: applySlashCommand=function alongside all other design symbols.

discardedWithReason.candidate: Bottom of chat.ts (lines 342-350) re-exports types and values from extension-less paths (`from "./approval"`, `from "./llm"`, `from "./payload-apply"`, `from "./phase-utils"`, `from "./session"`).
discardedWithReason.reason: Style inconsistency with the diff's other "./X.js" extensions, but each target file exists as a .ts file in design/, so tsx resolves the extension-less paths correctly. No runtime defect — verified by the barrel-resolution.test.ts passing and the workflow's own vitest suite (94 tests) running green.

discardedWithReason.candidate: workflows/feature-planning/src/index.ts and workflows/discovery/src/index.ts have the same extension-less inner-re-export pattern (`from "./plan-intent"`, `from "./discovery"`).
discardedWithReason.reason: Same resolution logic as chat.ts bottom: each target exists as a `.ts` file so tsx resolves them. Out of scope for this fix (only module-management was named in the task), and not broken at runtime. Flagging for the writing agent's awareness but not a defect.
Each target file exists as `.ts` so tsx resolves them. Pre-existing pattern not touched by the writer; works at runtime.

discardedWithReason.candidate: apps/mcp/package.json declares `@repo/module-management-workflow` as a dependency but no source file in apps/mcp imports from it.
discardedWithReason.reason: Unused dependency. Not a runtime defect — pnpm tolerates unused workspace deps without breaking the build. Housekeeping, not battle-test territory. The MCP server doesn't import any module-design symbols and therefore doesn't need a lazy-load invariant.

discardedWithReason.candidate: apps/cli/dist/index.js may have a top-level `init_module_design2` call that defeats the lazy-load boundary.
discardedWithReason.reason: Verified preserved. The dist contains two `init_module_design*` symbols: `init_module_design` for `packages/agent-engine/src/module-design.ts` (agent-engine's own module-design, which the CLI legitimately needs at boot) and `init_module_design2` for `src/commands/module-design/index.ts` (the CLI's lazy subgraph). `init_module_design2` is only invoked inside the `.action()` callbacks via `Promise.resolve().then(() => (init_module_design2(), module_design_exports))`. Confirmed by `node apps/cli/dist/index.js --help` / `list-modules --json` / `config list` all exiting 0.

discardedWithReason.candidate: The dynamic import string `await import("./commands/module-design/index.js")` might lack the `.js` extension and break under tsx.
discardedWithReason.reason: Verified: the CLI source uses `./commands/module-design/index.js` (with .js) in both the `module create` and `module consistency` action callbacks. Resolves correctly under tsx and is preserved by tsup in the dist. Already implicitly checked by the spec-writer's lazy-load-invariant.test.ts.
